---
title: "precinct_models"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## 5-Fold CV for 2020 (Predicting Trump Share)

precinct20_m1

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)
library(scales) # for comma labels in plots

# ==========================================
# 1. Load and Clean Data
# ==========================================

# Load the clean 2020 precinct dataset
clean_data <- read_csv("data/prep/precincts/2020/clean_emb_precinct_20.csv")

# Prepare data:
# Keep ID and Density for analysis, but we will EXCLUDE them from the model formula later.
data_rf <- clean_data %>%
  select(
    precinct_id, 
    vote_density, 
    TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
    ends_with("_mean") # Only embedding means
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("Data Loaded. Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "\n")

# ==========================================
# 2. Setup 5-Fold Cross-Validation
# ==========================================

set.seed(42)

# Create 5 random folds
# We shuffle the data first, then assign a fold number (1 through 5)
data_rf <- data_rf %>%
  mutate(fold_id = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n())))

# Initialize an empty list to store predictions
all_predictions <- list()

cat("Starting 5-Fold Cross-Validation on", nrow(data_rf), "precincts...\n")

# ==========================================
# 3. The CV Loop
# ==========================================

for(k in 1:5) {
  
  # A. Split Data
  train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
  test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
  
  # B. Train Model
  # Standard Regression Rule: mtry = Number of features / 3
  n_features <- ncol(train_df) - 4 # Subtract ID, density, share, fold_id
  # my_mtry    <- floor(n_features / 3)
  
  rf_model <- ranger(
    TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - vote_density - fold_id, 
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = 8, # my_mtry, # Dynamic (approx 21 for 64 embeddings)
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )
  
  # C. Predict
  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
  
  # D. Store Results
  test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
  all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
  
  cat("  Fold", k, "complete. Rows predicted:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
}

# Combine all 5 folds back into one big dataframe
full_results <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )

# save results
write_csv(full_results, "data/results/precinct20_m1_results.csv")

```

R-Squared: 0.735 RMSE: 0.1203 Mean Abs Error: 0.0885

## 5-Fold CV for 2024 (Predicting Trump Share)

Precinct24_m1

```{r}

# ==========================================
# 1. Load and Clean Data (2024)
# ==========================================

# Load the clean 2024 precinct dataset
clean_data <- read_csv("data/prep/precincts/2024/clean_emb_precinct_24.csv")

# Prepare data:
# Keep ID and Density for analysis, but we will EXCLUDE them from the model formula later.
data_rf <- clean_data %>%
  select(
    precinct_id, 
    vote_density, 
    TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
    ends_with("_mean") # Only embedding means
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("Data Loaded. 2024 Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "\n")

# ==========================================
# 2. Setup 5-Fold Cross-Validation
# ==========================================

set.seed(42)

# Create 5 random folds
# We shuffle the data first, then assign a fold number (1 through 5)
data_rf <- data_rf %>%
  mutate(fold_id = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n())))

# Initialize an empty list to store predictions
all_predictions <- list()

cat("Starting 5-Fold Cross-Validation on", nrow(data_rf), "precincts...\n")

# ==========================================
# 3. The CV Loop
# ==========================================

for(k in 1:5) {
  
  # A. Split Data
  train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
  test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
  
  # B. Train Model
  # Standard Regression Rule: mtry = Number of features / 3
  n_features <- ncol(train_df) - 4 # Subtract ID, density, share, fold_id
  # my_mtry    <- floor(n_features / 3)
  
  rf_model <- ranger(
    TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - vote_density - fold_id, 
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = 8, # my_mtry, # Dynamic (approx 21 for 64 embeddings)
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )
  
  # C. Predict
  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
  
  # D. Store Results
  test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
  all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
  
  cat("  Fold", k, "complete. Rows predicted:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
}

# Combine all 5 folds back into one big dataframe
full_results <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )

# Save results
write_csv(full_results, "data/results/precinct24_m1_results.csv")
```

R-Squared: 0.72 RMSE: 0.1174 Mean Abs Error: 0.0867

precinct20_m2

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)
library(scales) # for comma labels in plots

# ==========================================
# 1. Load and Clean Data
# ==========================================

# Load the clean 2020 precinct dataset
clean_data <- read_csv("data/prep/precincts/2020/clean_emb_precinct_20.csv")

# Prepare data:
# Keep ID, state, and density for analysis, but EXCLUDE them from the model formula later.
data_rf <- clean_data %>%
  select(
    precinct_id, 
    state,               # NEW: Must keep state to assign folds
    vote_density, 
    TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
    ends_with("_mean")   # Only embedding means
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("Data Loaded. Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "\n")

# ==========================================
# 2. Setup 5-Fold SPATIAL Cross-Validation
# ==========================================

set.seed(42)

# 1. Extract the unique states
unique_states <- unique(data_rf$state)

# 2. Shuffle the states and assign each one a fold (1 through 5)
state_folds <- tibble(
  state = sample(unique_states),
  fold_id = rep(1:5, length.out = length(unique_states))
)

# 3. Join the fold assignments back to the precinct data
data_rf <- data_rf %>%
  left_join(state_folds, by = "state")

# Quick check to see how many precincts are in each fold
cat("\nPrecincts per fold (Notice they won't be perfectly even anymore, which is fine!):\n")
print(table(data_rf$fold_id))

# Initialize an empty list to store predictions
all_predictions <- list()

cat("\nStarting 5-Fold State-Level Cross-Validation on", nrow(data_rf), "precincts...\n")

# ==========================================
# 3. The CV Loop
# ==========================================

for(k in 1:5) {
  
  # A. Split Data
  train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
  test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
  
  # B. Train Model
  # We exclude precinct_id, state, vote_density, and fold_id from the predictors
  rf_model <- ranger(
    TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - state - vote_density - fold_id, 
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = 8, 
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )
  
  # C. Predict
  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
  
  # D. Store Results
  test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
  all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
  
  cat("  Fold", k, "complete. States tested:", length(unique(test_df$state)), "| Rows predicted:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
}

# Combine all 5 folds back into one big dataframe
full_results_spatial <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )

# save results
write_csv(full_results_spatial, "data/results/precinct20_m2_results.csv")
```

precinct24_m2

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)
library(scales) # for comma labels in plots

# ==========================================
# 1. Load and Clean Data
# ==========================================

# Load the clean 2020 precinct dataset
clean_data <- read_csv("data/prep/precincts/2024/clean_emb_precinct_24.csv")

# Prepare data:
# Keep ID, state, and density for analysis, but EXCLUDE them from the model formula later.
data_rf <- clean_data %>%
  select(
    precinct_id, 
    state,               # NEW: Must keep state to assign folds
    vote_density, 
    TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
    ends_with("_mean")   # Only embedding means
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("Data Loaded. Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "\n")

# ==========================================
# 2. Setup 5-Fold SPATIAL Cross-Validation
# ==========================================

set.seed(42)

# 1. Extract the unique states
unique_states <- unique(data_rf$state)

# 2. Shuffle the states and assign each one a fold (1 through 5)
state_folds <- tibble(
  state = sample(unique_states),
  fold_id = rep(1:5, length.out = length(unique_states))
)

# 3. Join the fold assignments back to the precinct data
data_rf <- data_rf %>%
  left_join(state_folds, by = "state")

# Quick check to see how many precincts are in each fold
cat("\nPrecincts per fold (Notice they won't be perfectly even anymore, which is fine!):\n")
print(table(data_rf$fold_id))

# Initialize an empty list to store predictions
all_predictions <- list()

cat("\nStarting 5-Fold State-Level Cross-Validation on", nrow(data_rf), "precincts...\n")

# ==========================================
# 3. The CV Loop
# ==========================================

for(k in 1:5) {
  
  # A. Split Data
  train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
  test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
  
  # B. Train Model
  # We exclude precinct_id, state, vote_density, and fold_id from the predictors
  rf_model <- ranger(
    TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - state - vote_density - fold_id, 
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = 8, 
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )
  
  # C. Predict
  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
  
  # D. Store Results
  test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
  all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
  
  cat("  Fold", k, "complete. States tested:", length(unique(test_df$state)), "| Rows predicted:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
}

# Combine all 5 folds back into one big dataframe
full_results_spatial <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )

# save results
write_csv(full_results_spatial, "data/results/precinct24_m2_results.csv")
```

Other random spatial models, run using a function

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)
library(scales) # for comma labels in plots

# ==========================================
# 1. Define the Modeling Function
# ==========================================
# This function applies your exact 5-fold CV logic but dynamically changes 
# the data and predictors based on the model we want to run.

run_cv_model <- function(data_file, output_file, use_embeddings = TRUE) {
  
  cat("\n==========================================\n")
  cat("RUNNING MODEL -> Output:", output_file, "\n")
  cat("==========================================\n")
  
  # A. Load the data
  clean_data <- read_csv(data_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  # B. Prepare data and define the formula based on the model type
  if (use_embeddings) {
    # MODEL M4: Embeddings + area_km2 + vote_density
    data_rf <- clean_data %>%
      select(
        precinct_id, 
        area_km2,
        vote_density, 
        TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
        ends_with("_mean") 
      ) %>%
      na.omit()
    
    # Formula uses everything except ID and fold_id
    rf_formula <- TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - fold_id
    my_mtry <- 8 # Same as your original embedding model
    
  } else {
    # MODEL M3: area_km2 + vote_density ONLY
    data_rf <- clean_data %>%
      select(
        precinct_id, 
        area_km2,
        vote_density, 
        TRUMPSHARE = rep_share
      ) %>%
      na.omit()
    
    # Formula explicitly uses only the two baseline predictors
    rf_formula <- TRUMPSHARE ~ vote_density + area_km2
    my_mtry <- 1 # Standard rule (p/3) for 2 features
  }
  
  cat("Data Loaded. Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "| Features included:", ifelse(use_embeddings, "Embeddings + Baseline", "Baseline ONLY"), "\n")
  
  # C. Setup 5-Fold Cross-Validation
  set.seed(42)
  data_rf <- data_rf %>%
    mutate(fold_id = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n())))
  
  all_predictions <- list()
  
  # D. The CV Loop
  for(k in 1:5) {
    
    train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
    test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
    
    rf_model <- ranger(
      formula    = rf_formula, 
      data       = train_df,
      num.trees  = 500,
      mtry       = my_mtry, 
      importance = "impurity",
      seed       = 42
    )
    
    # Predict and Store
    preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
    test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
    all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
    
    cat("  Fold", k, "complete.\n")
  }
  
  # E. Combine Folds and Calculate Errors
  full_results <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
    mutate(
      Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
      Abs_Error = abs(Error)
    )
  
  # F. Save Results
  write_csv(full_results, output_file)
  cat("-> Saved to:", output_file, "\n\n")
}

# ==========================================
# 2. Execute the 4 Models
# ==========================================

# Define file paths
data_2020 <- "data/prep/precincts/2020/clean_emb_precinct_20.csv"
data_2024 <- "data/prep/precincts/2024/clean_emb_precinct_24.csv"

# ----- 2020 MODELS -----
# M4: Embeddings + area_km2 + vote_density
run_cv_model(
  data_file = data_2020, 
  output_file = "data/results/precinct20_m4_results.csv", 
  use_embeddings = TRUE
)

# M3: area_km2 + vote_density ONLY
run_cv_model(
  data_file = data_2020, 
  output_file = "data/results/precinct20_m3_results.csv", 
  use_embeddings = FALSE
)

# ----- 2024 MODELS -----
# M4: Embeddings + area_km2 + vote_density
run_cv_model(
  data_file = data_2024, 
  output_file = "data/results/precinct24_m4_results.csv", 
  use_embeddings = TRUE
)

# M3: area_km2 + vote_density ONLY
run_cv_model(
  data_file = data_2024, 
  output_file = "data/results/precinct24_m3_results.csv", 
  use_embeddings = FALSE
)

cat("All 4 models successfully trained and saved!\n")
```

Other leave-out-state CV models, run using a function

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)

# ==========================================
# 1. Define the Spatial Precinct Modeling Function
# ==========================================

run_precinct_spatial_model <- function(data_file, year_label, model_type) {
  
  output_file <- paste0("data/results/precinct", year_label, "_", model_type, "_results.csv")
  
  cat("\n==========================================\n")
  cat("RUNNING PRECINCT SPATIAL MODEL:", toupper(model_type), "| YEAR:", year_label, "\n")
  cat("Output ->", output_file, "\n")
  cat("==========================================\n")
  
  # A. Load the data
  clean_data <- read_csv(data_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  # B. Prepare data based on Model Type
  if (model_type == "m6") {
    # M6: Embeddings + area_km2 + vote_density
    data_rf <- clean_data %>%
      select(
        precinct_id, 
        state,             # Needed for spatial CV folds
        TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
        area_km2,          # Included as predictor
        vote_density,      # Included as predictor
        ends_with("_mean") # Included as predictors
      ) %>%
      na.omit()
      
  } else if (model_type == "m5") {
    # M5: Baseline Spatial ONLY (area_km2 + vote_density)
    data_rf <- clean_data %>%
      select(
        precinct_id, 
        state, 
        TRUMPSHARE = rep_share, 
        area_km2,          # Included as predictor
        vote_density       # Included as predictor
      ) %>%
      na.omit()
  }
  
  # C. Formula and Feature Count
  # We subtract precinct_id, state, and fold_id so they aren't used to predict.
  # Notice we DO NOT subtract area_km2 or vote_density here, because we want the model to use them!
  rf_formula <- TRUMPSHARE ~ . - precinct_id - state - fold_id
  n_features <- ncol(data_rf) - 3 # Subtract precinct_id, state, share
  
  # Calculate mtry (Standard regression rule: features / 3, minimum of 1)
  my_mtry <- min(8, max(1, floor(n_features / 3)))
  
  # D. Setup 5-Fold SPATIAL Cross-Validation
  set.seed(42)
  unique_states <- unique(data_rf$state)
  
  state_folds <- tibble(
    state = sample(unique_states),
    fold_id = rep(1:5, length.out = length(unique_states))
  )
  
  data_rf <- data_rf %>% left_join(state_folds, by = "state")
  
  cat("Data Prepared. Precincts:", nrow(data_rf), "| States:", length(unique_states), "\n")
  cat("Predictors:", n_features, "| mtry:", my_mtry, "\n")
  
  all_predictions <- list()
  
  # E. The CV Loop
  for(k in 1:5) {
    
    train_df <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id != k)
    test_df  <- data_rf %>% filter(fold_id == k)
    
    rf_model <- ranger(
      formula    = rf_formula, 
      data       = train_df,
      num.trees  = 500,
      mtry       = my_mtry,
      importance = "impurity",
      seed       = 42
    )
    
    # Predict and Store
    preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_df)$predictions
    test_df$Predicted_Share <- preds
    all_predictions[[k]] <- test_df
    
    states_tested <- length(unique(test_df$state))
    cat("  Fold", k, "complete. States tested:", states_tested, "| Rows predicted:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
  }
  
  # F. Combine Folds and Calculate Errors
  full_results <- bind_rows(all_predictions) %>%
    mutate(
      Error = Predicted_Share - TRUMPSHARE,
      Abs_Error = abs(Error)
    )
  
  # G. Save Results
  write_csv(full_results, output_file)
  cat("-> Saved successfully to", output_file, "\n")
}

# ==========================================
# 2. Execute All 4 Models
# ==========================================

# Define file paths
file_20 <- "data/prep/precincts/2020/clean_emb_precinct_20.csv"
file_24 <- "data/prep/precincts/2024/clean_emb_precinct_24.csv"

# Map years to files
years <- list("20" = file_20, "24" = file_24)

# Models M5 and M6
models <- c("m5", "m6")

# Loop through both years and both models
for (yr_label in names(years)) {
  for (mod in models) {
    run_precinct_spatial_model(
      data_file = years[[yr_label]], 
      year_label = yr_label, 
      model_type = mod
    )
  }
}

cat("\nAll 4 Precinct-Level Spatial CV models successfully trained and saved!\n")
```

## Results and Visualization

```{r}
# ==========================================
# 4. Overall Performance Metrics
# ==========================================

#full_results <- read_csv("data/results/precinct20_m1_results.csv")
full_results <- read_csv("data/results/precinct24_m6_results.csv")

# Calculate metrics on the full dataset (all folds combined)
rss  <- sum(full_results$Error^2)
tss  <- sum((full_results$TRUMPSHARE - mean(full_results$TRUMPSHARE))^2)
r2   <- 1 - (rss / tss)
rmse <- sqrt(mean(full_results$Error^2))

cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nOVERALL PRECINCT CV RESULTS")
cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nR-Squared: ", round(r2, 3))
cat("\nRMSE:      ", round(rmse, 4))
cat("\nMean Abs Error:", round(mean(full_results$Abs_Error), 4))
cat("\n==========================================\n")

# ==========================================
# 5. Plot 1: Prediction Error vs. Density
# ==========================================

# Using log10 scale because precinct density varies wildly
plot1 <- ggplot(full_results, aes(x = vote_density, y = Error)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.2) + # High transparency for dense data
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) + 
  labs(
    title = "Precinct Prediction Error vs. Voter Density (5-Fold CV)",
    subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(r2, 3), " | RMSE = ", round(rmse, 3)),
    x = "Voters per km² (Log Scale)",
    y = "Error (Predicted - Actual)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot1)

# ==========================================
# 6. Plot 2: R-squared per Density Decile
# ==========================================

decile_stats <- full_results %>%
  mutate(density_decile = ntile(vote_density, 10)) %>%
  group_by(density_decile) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_density = median(vote_density),
    n = n(),
    RSS = sum(Error^2),
    TSS = sum((TRUMPSHARE - mean(TRUMPSHARE))^2),
    # Safety check: avoid dividing by zero if TSS is 0
    R2 = if_else(TSS > 1e-6, 1 - (RSS / TSS), 0)
  )

plot2 <- ggplot(decile_stats, aes(x = factor(density_decile), y = R2)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(R2, 2)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Performance by Density Decile (Precincts)",
    subtitle = "Decile 1 = Most Rural, Decile 10 = Most Urban",
    x = "Density Decile",
    y = "R-squared"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot2)

# ==========================================
# 7. Plot 3: Predicted vs. Actual
# ==========================================

# We use a 2D density heatmap (geom_bin2d) because there are too many 
# precincts for a standard scatter plot to be readable.
plot3 <- ggplot(full_results, aes(x = TRUMPSHARE, y = Predicted_Share)) +
  # Create a heatmap of point density
  geom_bin2d(bins = 100) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", trans = "log10") + # Log scale help see low-density areas
  # Add the "Perfect Prediction" line
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "white", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  # Add a trend line to see if the model under/over predicts at the extremes
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "cyan", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Actual vs. Predicted Trump Share (Precinct Level)",
    subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(r2, 3), " | Dashed line represents perfect prediction"),
    x = "Actual Vote Share",
    y = "Predicted Vote Share",
    fill = "Precinct Count\n(Log Scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(plot3)

```
